title: Groenland–Joret–Nadara–Walczak Conjecture on treedepth vs. pathwidth
statement_text: Every graph of pathwidth less than $a$ containing no $2^b$-vertex path has treedepth $O(ab)$.
context_text: Theorem 1 (Czerwiński, Nadara, Pilipczuk) had previously given a bound of $O(a^2 b)$ for such graphs by combining treewidth and binary-tree-minor bounds. The authors of [5] conjectured the bound could be reduced to $O(ab)$, matching the natural product of the two parameters.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture by establishing Theorem 3: every graph of pathwidth less than $a$ containing no $2^b$-vertex path has treedepth at most $10ab$, and shows this bound is tight up to a constant factor.
notes: Reference [5] is Groenland, Joret, Nadara, Walczak; year could not be confirmed from the extracted text. PDF source — exponent notation (2^b vs 2b) inferred from context.
paper_title: Tight bound on treedepth in terms of pathwidth and longest path

title: Open Problem: sharpness of the treedepth bound in Theorem 1
statement_text: Whether the bound $O(thb)$ in Theorem 1 — that every graph of treewidth less than $t$ with no complete binary tree of height $h$ as a minor and no $2^b$-vertex path has treedepth $O(thb)$ — can be improved remains an open problem.
context_text: Theorems 2 and 3 together imply Theorem 1, and their individual bounds are each sharp up to constant factors. However, it is unknown whether the combined bound $O(thb)$ in Theorem 1 is optimal or can be reduced.
notes: Stated as prose without a labelled theorem environment. PDF source — exponent notation inferred from context.
paper_title: Tight bound on treedepth in terms of pathwidth and longest path
